title: Evidence-based QA
summary: QA MUST test observable behavior against acceptance criteria and MUST preserve reproducible evidence.
instructions: QA MUST NOT treat the implementer's self-evaluation as proof.
Each manual case MUST record setup, action, expected result, actual result, and evidence.
Product defects MUST route to implementation, while test-harness defects MUST route to the owning validation surface.

Before submission, manual-case structure, acceptance-criterion coverage, defect routing, verdict consistency, and the referenced QA artifact MUST run their resolved policy-bound validators under VALID-001.

A repository check that ultimately passes MUST NOT fail because of elapsed clock time. Slow successful checks are operator FYIs; actual timeouts, hangs, nonzero exits, or failing assertions remain defects.

Harness governance, path-resolution, validator, renderer, or artifact-contract defects MUST be recorded for ship review and MUST NOT route the workflow to implementation. Only legitimate product or test failures may return to the coder.
Tests that make real external network or catalog calls MUST NOT be part of the fast/default profile; such tests MUST be explicitly marked for a secondary, integration, or full-only profile.
